news.title: ‘It was crap’: Wayne Rooney’s candour helps BBC in World Cup final TV battle
news.lede: BBC scored a late goal in head-to-head with ITV over the World Cup by being in the stadium for the final. “I thought it was crap,” Wayne Rooney surmised on BBC after enduring the half-time show. Amid a tense final, the interval was always going to be prominent, theoretically bringing light to the shade. Why the two UK broadcasters felt the need to transmit the karaoke session in full we will never know but at least the pundits struggled through it, too.

news.title: Spain 1-0 Argentina (aet): 2026 World Cup final player ratings
news.lede: Rodri looks back to his imperious best in Spain’s midfield while Enzo Fernández has a day to forget for Argentina. Unai Simón Was alert to clear danger after Cubarsí’s early mistake. Otherwise could have brought his slippers. Pedro Porro Doesn’t look like a player who was in a relegation battle a few weeks ago.

news.title: Spain win the World Cup after Ferran Torres dethrones 10-man Argentina in extra time
news.lede: Enzo Fernández sent off for two bookings in normal time. Victory secures second men’s World Cup title for La Roja. Spain finally broke the dogged resistance of 10-man Argentina in extra time to win the men’s World Cup for the second time as a drab final ended in a dramatic fashion. The Barcelona winger Ferran Torres scored the only goal of a disjointed game in the 106th minute, with Argentina’s cynical approach summed up by a mass confrontation sparked by Leandro Paredes pushing Gavi and Eric García to the ground after the final whistle, which led to the Boca Juniors midfielder following Enzo Fernández in being shown a red card.

news.title: Hits, misses and Muppets: the star-studded World Cup half-time show nobody asked for
news.lede: It zipped along, cramming in Shakira, Madonna and Coldplay, but football fans may not be convinced of need for this mid-match spectacle. Like hydration breaks and Donald Trump’s ability to get refereeing decisions overturned by simply phoning up the president of Fifa and complaining, the final half-time show is an innovation of the 2026 World Cup that comes with a degree of controversy attached. For one thing, its existence automatically means the World Cup final breaks the International Football Association’s 15-minute half-time mandate. For another, its obvious antecedent lies in the NFL’s Super Bowl, where the half-time entertainment has mushroomed over the decades from a few marching bands to one of the big pop events of the year.

news.title: Lionel Messi’s World Cup fairytale ends with pitiful Argentina as final villains | Pablo Iglesias Maurer
news.lede: Game’s greatest player will not see his legacy sullied by defeat to Spain but his team’s display destroyed the game. Playing on the grandest stage in sport for the last time, Lionel Messi seemed almost certain to provide one last memorable moment in this World Cup final. Yet as the sun ducked behind the west stand of New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday, it became clearer and clearer that Argentina had lost the magic they had carried through this tournament. And as Spain gradually wrested control, one was left to confront reality: Messi, and Argentina, had finally run out of time, having long since given in to their worst instincts in a 1-0 defeat.

news.title: Spain secure World Cup glory to banish miserable Argentina and their anti-football
news.lede: Spain win a second star after finally beating a black hole of an Argentina team in the World Cup final. Lionel Messi may not have got the ending that he and much of the world may have wanted but few can say the winners weren’t the right champions for the World Cup final. Spain have been the best team and they were duly the only side actually playing to win this otherwise dismal occasion across more than 120 minutes. It had an argument to be the worst final of all - which subsequently descended into chaos after Leandro Paredes’ antics in the final whistle - until Ferran Torres rose above it all to send the ball high into the Argentina net.

news.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
news.lede: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final.
